name: paid_cpa_qualified_conversion
definition: Total ad spend divided by qualified conversions (trial start, demo booked, or purchase — not unqualified signup) in the attribution window.
target_hint: B2B SaaS: $50–$200 CPA for qualified trial/demo is typical early stage. Ecommerce: target 3:1 ROAS minimum. Pause and fix if CPA >2× target for 7 days.

name: paid_conversion_rate
definition: Qualified conversions divided by ad-attributed landing page sessions.
target_hint: 3–8% for dedicated LP with message match. <2% means LP or audience problem — fix before scaling spend.

name: paid_creative_ctr
definition: Click-through rate on ad creative, tracked per variant.
target_hint: Search: 3–6% CTR on branded, 1–3% on category. Social: 0.8–2% CTR cold, 2–4% retargeting. CTR drop >30% week-over-week = creative fatigue.

name: paid_hypothesis_win_rate
definition: Percentage of structured A/B tests where variant beats control on primary metric with pre-defined confidence (e.g. 50+ conversions per variant).
target_hint: 30–40% win rate means healthy testing cadence. <15% means hypotheses are too random; >60% means tests aren't bold enough.

name: paid_trial_to_paid_rate
definition: Percentage of ad-attributed trials or signups that convert to paid within 30 days.
target_hint: 10–25% for PLG B2B. <8% means ads bring wrong audience or onboarding fails — don't scale until this holds.
